feed.title: Karubits
feed.subtitle: Karubits, A collection of guides and notes focused on Infrastructure, System Administration, Cloud Engineering and Devops workflows.

entry.title: Proxmox cloud-init templates
entry.summary: Introduction and why TODO Download cloud templates of your preferred distributions Listed below are the official download links for Debian and Ubuntu release Distro Release Download Checksum Debian 11 Bullseye (Backports) 💿 🔑 Debian 11 Bullseye 💿 🔑 Debian 10 Buste...

entry.title: Firmware upgrade Intel DC SSD on Linux

link.title: Firmware upgrade Intel DC SSD on Linux

entry.summary: Server Preperation / Hardware Notes Intel Datacenter SSD Firmware At the time of writting, Intel’s NAND SSD business has been acquired by Solidigm. The tooling which was previously called Intel MAS is no longed supported. This example will cover upgrading the firwmare of Intel DC series firmware on a Debian operating system. This same steps are applicable to Proxmox hypervisors as well. ...

entry.title: RKE2 HA Deployement with no external Load Balancer

link.title: RKE2 HA Deployement with no external Load Balancer

entry.summary: RKE2 HA Deployement with no external Load Balancer This article is still a mess. Please don’t use it, they are rough notes. I will be publishing a new article soon on K3S + KUBE-VIP + Longhorn + MetalLB First setup some environmental varibles. The top three lines should be customized to your own environment. # Environment Varibles export VIP=10.11.240.210 # <- Load balancer virtual IP ...

entry.title: Linux VNC Server
entry.summary: How to setup a VNC Server on Linux On the PC / Desktop you want to access Install the X11 Server sudo apt install -y x11vnc Then setup the initial password x11vnc -usepw Press Ctrl+C to close the vnc server session. On the remote PC You can add the command to start the vnc server on the remote PC in your ~/.ssh/config as below. This approach means the vn...

entry.title: Linux Storage Notes
entry.summary: Linux Storage Notes A collection of rough notes around managing storage in Linux 1. Drive sanitization NIST Publiciation 800-88 Rev 1 - Guidelines for Media Sanitization 1.1 Secure Erase for NVME Drive Install the nvme-cli tools sudo apt install -y nvme-cli confirm which disk to target lsblk -o name,model,serial,type | grep nvme | grep disk or nvme list Perform a crytographic er...
